message:
test(fcp-core): pin TrustLevel ordering + RiskLevel discriminant pinning (flywheel_connectors-ljnej)

Bead asks for IntentPriority ordering (Low<Medium<High<Critical)
plus partial_cmp. fcp-core has no type literally named
IntentPriority. The closest 4-variant Low/Medium/High/Critical type
is RiskLevel (capability.rs:2254) BUT it does NOT derive PartialOrd
or Ord, so the bead's partial_cmp ask is structurally impossible
there.

The closest ORDERED priority-shaped classifier is TrustLevel
(capability.rs:2792, 6 variants Blocked<Anonymous<Untrusted<Paired<
Admin<Owner) which DOES derive PartialOrd+Ord with documented
"lowest to highest trust" ordering.

Coverage:
- TrustLevel strict ordering chain pinned across all 6 variants
- cmp() truth table per pair (36 cases via index comparison)
- partial_cmp returns Some(_) for every pair (total ordering, no
  None) — directly addresses the bead's partial_cmp ask
- max / min / sort behave consistently with `<`
- Order properties: reflexivity, antisymmetry, transitivity across
  the full 6-element chain
- Per-variant JSON tag pinned (lowercase via rename_all=lowercase:
  blocked/anonymous/untrusted/paired/admin/owner)
- PascalCase + unknown rejected
- JSON + CBOR round-trip per variant
- CBOR round-trip preserves relative ordering across the full chain
- 6-variant count sentinel
- RiskLevel mismatch documented: variant names match the bead's
  Low/Medium/High/Critical pattern but no Ord derive — discriminant
  order via `as u8` is the only observable ordering surface and
  follows source declaration order (Low=0, Medium=1, High=2,
  Critical=3); RiskLevel serde tags pinned in declaration order
  to catch silent reorderings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
